questions.question: Why does a compass needle point north?
answers: The earth is like a magnet and needle points to the north pole
It always points away from the sun
Because of the pull of the 2 nearest planets

questions.question: Why does mercury rise in a thermometer?
answers: It increases in volume when the temperature rises
It rises when air pressure drops
It rises as air pressure rises

questions.question: How does blood circulate around the body?
answers: It is pumped by the heart
By movement of the body
It flows from the lungs as you breathe

questions.question: We hear an echo when ...
answers: Sound is bounced back from an object
We are in a cave
Everything is silent

questions.question: How do insects breathe?
answers: Through holes known as spiracles
Through their noses
Through their mouths

questions.question: Why do we hear a radio?
answers: Sound signals are transmitted through space
We switch it on
We plug it in

questions.question: Why can we see distant objects better with a telescope?
answers: A lens magnifier produces a magnified image
The image is compressed through a small opening
It brings them nearer

questions.question: Why does a candlewick continue to burn?
answers: The wick is saturated with wax
It is enclosed in a cylinder of wax
It is made from special material

questions.question: How is water from a fire engine propelled to a great height?
answers: By a pump on the engine
By extending the hosepipe
by adjusting the nozzle

questions.question: Why do balloons filled with helium rise?
answers: Helium is lighter than air
Helium is a very energetic gas
Helium propels them
